title: Separation of free virus particles from sediments in aquatic systems

guidelines:
{"blocks":[{"key":"c30vt","text":"The optimized protocol for the separation of free virus particles from sediments is shown in Figure 1.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"eq3gc","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d0k1b","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4iq58","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2h0m3","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":[]},{"key":"edho7","text":"
Figure 1: Protocol illustrating the steps required for the separation of viruses from the sediment particle and subsequent counting. 

References

Danovaro, R., A. Dell’Anno, A. Trucco, and S. Vannucci. 2001. Determination of virus abundance in marine sediments. Appl. Environ. Microbiol. 67:1384-1387.

Duhamel, S., and S. Jacquet. 2006. Flow cytometric analysis of bacteria and virus-like particles in lake sediments. J. Microbiol. Met. 64:316-322

Fischer, U. R., A. K. T. Kirschner, and B. Velimirov. 2005. Optimization of extraction and estimation of viruses in silty freshwater sediments. Aquat. Microb. Ecol. 40:207-216.

Hewson, I. and J. A. Fuhrman. 2003. Viriobenthos production and virioplankton sorptive scavenging by suspended sediment particles in coastal and pelagic waters. Microb. Ecol. 46:337-347.

Middelboe, M., R. N. Glud, and K. Finster. 2003. Distribution of viruses and bacteria in relation to diagenetic activity in an estuarine sediment. Limnol. Oceanogr. 48:1447-1456.

Middelboe, M.,  R. N. Glud 2006. Viral activity along a trophic gradient in continental margin sediments off central Chile. Mar. Biol. Res. 2:41-51.

Middelboe, M.,  R. N. Glud., F. Wenzhöfer, K. Oguri, and H. Kitazato. 2006. Spatial distribution and activity of viruses in the deep-sea sediments of Sagami Bay, Japan. Deep-Sea Res. 53:1-13.

Noble, R. T., and J. A. Fuhrman. 1998. Use of SYBR Green I for rapid epifluorescence counts of marine viruses and bacteria. Aquat. Microb. Ecol. 14:113-118.

Patel, A., R. T. Noble, J. A. Steele, M. S. Schwalbach, I. Hewson, and J. A. Fuhrman. 2007. Virus and prokaryote enumeration from planktonic aquatic environments by epifluorescence microscopy with SYBR Green I. Nature Protocols 2:269-276.

Suttle, C. A. and J. A. Fuhrman. 2010. Enumeration of virus particles in aquatic or sediment samples by epifluorescence microscopy, p. 145-153. In S. W. Wilhelm, M. G. Weinbauer, and C. A. Suttle [eds.], Manual of Aquatic Viral Ecology. ASLO.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":1,"length":9},{"style":"bold","offset":136,"length":10},{"style":"italic","offset":136,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"imageblock","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"FB2AD51BBB2E4BF2A3474FCE40D5E575","height":null,"is_video":false,"mime":null,"original_name":"Figure 1.jpg","placeholder":"https://content.protocols.io/2nc2w6.jpg","source":"https://content.protocols.io/2nc2w6.jpg","width":null}}}}
steps: Create sediment slurries by combining 0.5 mL sediment and 4.5 mL virus-free water.
Add tetrasodium pyrophosphate (5 mM final concentration).
Sonicate a second time at 47 KHz for 1 min. 00:01:00
Manually shake a second time for 30 sec. 00:00:30
Sonicate a third time at 47 KHz for 1 min. 00:01:00
Manually shake a third time for 30 sec. 00:00:30
Dilute supernatant in virus-free seawater (from 10x to 1000x).
creator: Mathias Middelboe
